subject: Mathematics
title: The Pythagorean Theorem
description: A quick, practical guide to The Pythagorean Theorem.

hook.text: In any right triangle, the square on the longest side equals the sum of the other two squares.

buildup.text: If the legs are 3 and 4, the hypotenuse is 5. Because 9 + 16 = 25.

discovery.text: This relationship was known to Babylonians 1,000 years before Pythagoras was born.

twist.text: There are over 400 known proofs. A US president, James Garfield, even published one.

climax.text: It's the foundation of distance formulas, trigonometry, and GPS satellite positioning.

punchline.text: Three squares, one right angle, and all of geometry unfolds.

quiz.question: What does the Pythagorean Theorem relate?
options: The sides of a right triangle
